GV.OC-04.subcategory: Critical objectives, capabilities, and services that stakeholders depend on or expect from the organization are understood and communicated

GV.OC-02.subcategory: Internal and external stakeholders are understood, and their needs and expectations regarding cybersecurity risk management are understood and considered

GV.RM-01.subcategory: Risk management objectives are established and agreed to by organizational stakeholders

GV.PO-01.subcategory: Policy for managing cybersecurity risks is established based on organizational context, cybersecurity strategy, and priorities and is communicated and enforced

GV.PO-02.subcategory: Policy for managing cybersecurity risks is reviewed, updated, communicated, and enforced to reflect changes in requirements, threats, technology, and organizational mission

ID.RA-02.subcategory: Cyber threat intelligence is received from information sharing forums and sources

GV.SC-02.subcategory: Cybersecurity roles and responsibilities for suppliers, customers, and partners are established, communicated, and coordinated internally and externally

GV.OC-03.subcategory: Legal, regulatory, and contractual requirements regarding cybersecurity - including privacy and civil liberties obligations - are understood and managed

GV.RR-03.subcategory: Adequate resources are allocated commensurate with the cybersecurity risk strategy, roles, responsibilities, and policies

GV.RR-02.subcategory: Roles, responsibilities, and authorities related to cybersecurity risk management are established, communicated, understood, and enforced

PR.AT-01.subcategory: Personnel are provided with awareness and training so that they possess the knowledge and skills to perform general tasks with cybersecurity risks in mind

GV.RR-04.subcategory: Cybersecurity is included in human resources practices

PR.AT-02.subcategory: Individuals in specialized roles are provided with awareness and training so that they possess the knowledge and skills to perform relevant tasks with cybersecurity risks in mind

ID.IM-02.subcategory: Improvements are identified from security tests and exercises, including those done in coordination with suppliers and relevant third parties

ID.RA-03.subcategory: Internal and external threats to the organization are identified and recorded

PR.IR-04.subcategory: Adequate resource capacity to ensure availability is maintained

GV.SC-04.subcategory: Suppliers are known and prioritized by criticality

PR.IR-03.subcategory: Mechanisms are implemented to achieve resilience requirements in normal and adverse situations

PR.DS-11.subcategory: Backups of data are created, protected, maintained, and tested

ID.IM-04.subcategory: Incident response plans and other cybersecurity plans that affect operations are established, communicated, maintained, and improved

PR.IR-01.subcategory: Networks and environments are protected from unauthorized logical access and usage

PR.AA-05.category: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
PR.AA-05.subcategory: Access permissions, entitlements, and authorizations are defined in a policy, managed, enforced, and reviewed, and incorporate the principles of least privilege and separation of duties

PR.PS-04.subcategory: Log records are generated and made available for continuous monitoring

DE.CM-01.subcategory: Networks and network services are monitored to find potentially adverse events

DE.CM-02.subcategory: The physical environment is monitored to find potentially adverse events

DE.CM-03.subcategory: Personnel activity and technology usage are monitored to find potentially adverse events

DE.CM-09.subcategory: Computing hardware and software, runtime environments, and their data are monitored to find potentially adverse events

DE.CM-06.subcategory: External service provider activities and services are monitored to find potentially adverse events

DE.AE-03.subcategory: Information is correlated from multiple sources

DE.AE-06.subcategory: Information on adverse events is provided to authorized staff and tools

ID.AM-08.subcategory: Systems, hardware, software, services, and data are managed throughout their life cycles

PR.PS-03.subcategory: Hardware is maintained, replaced, and removed commensurate with risk

PR.PS-02.subcategory: Software is maintained, replaced, and removed commensurate with risk

ID.IM-03.subcategory: Improvements are identified from execution of operational processes, procedures, and activities

RS.CO-02.subcategory: Internal and external stakeholders are notified of incidents

RS.CO-03.subcategory: Information is shared with designated internal and external stakeholders

RS.MA-01.subcategory: The incident response plan is executed in coordination with relevant third parties once an incident is declared

RS.MA-02.subcategory: Incident reports are triaged and validated

RS.AN-03.subcategory: Analysis is performed to establish what has taken place during an incident and the root cause of the incident

DE.AE-02.subcategory: Potentially adverse events are analyzed to better understand associated activities

ID.RA-01.subcategory: Vulnerabilities in assets are identified, validated, and recorded

GV.OV-03.subcategory: Organizational cybersecurity risk management performance is evaluated and reviewed for adjustments needed

PR.DS-01.subcategory: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-at-rest are protected

PR.DS-02.subcategory: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-transit are protected
